project.tagline: A Rich-powered CLI for versioned Postgres tables — snapshot, activate, retire without touching live reads.

emphasis: invariant-by-transaction design (activation flip enforces 'one active per type' without triggers)
dry-run as a first-class mode, not an afterthought
SQL-side atomic version allocation (COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) + 1 inside the database, not Python)
schema cloning via CREATE TABLE ... (LIKE source INCLUDING ALL) + chunked bulk fill with Rich progress
Rich TUI treatment for what is fundamentally a psql-with-superpowers tool
skip: any discussion of product-db-pipeline beyond what's needed to situate the table manager

pages.purpose: Single standalone component page — cli-first showcase of the Table Version Manager CLI. Hero invocation, command catalog, mechanism deep-dives, scope/boundary note referencing DBConnector + table_versions registry.

references.note: No existing DevDeck page is a close stylistic match for a cli-first component showcase. Research should not try to mimic project1 (narrative pipeline) or project2 (interactive island). This page becomes its own cli-first template: hero invocation → command catalog → mechanism deep-dives → scope/boundary note.

touchpointsToLocateButNotFullyDocument: src/database/db_connector.py (or wherever DBConnector lives) — just enough to describe the connection shape in one paragraph
the table_versions registry table — its schema origin (migration file, if discoverable) and columns
scopeBoundary.note: Intermediate depth requires a one-paragraph sketch of the two touchpoints above so the final page isn't amputated. Full documentation of DBConnector or the migration layer is out of scope.

provisionalNote: Emphasis, skip, and references not yet fully approved by operator. Research proceeding against best-reading-of-conversation brief. Operator will finalize brief after reviewing research output; a second research pass may follow if emphasis shifts materially.
